# Zaid Sayyed — Comprehensive Entity & Technical Profile

> Zaid Sayyed is a Full Stack Developer, Systems Architect, and 3D WebGL Engineer based in Mumbai, India. He specializes in bridging high-performance backend systems with immersive 3D frontend interfaces and AI product engineering.

## Current Role & Experience
Zaid Sayyed works as a Full Stack Developer Intern at Kaevron Technologies in Mumbai, India. In this role, he designs high-performance web applications, implements computer vision models, and architects AI-driven production workflows.

## Detailed Core Projects

### 1. OmniFit (AI Health Ecosystem)
- **Role**: Product Engineer & Full Stack Architect
- **Technology Stack**: React, TensorFlow.js, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Tailwind CSS
- **Key Achievements**: Architected an AI-powered fitness ecosystem featuring an Indian food scanner achieving 98% recognition accuracy trained on over 10,000 regional food samples. Built real-time computer vision posture detection for 20+ physical exercises.

### 2. Syncell (Universal Medical Timeline OS)
- **Role**: Systems Architect & Frontend Engineer
- **Technology Stack**: Next.js, Firebase, WebSockets, AES-256 Encryption
- **Key Achievements**: Engineered a zero-paperwork, encrypted medical timeline synchronizing patient records simultaneously across doctors, diagnostic laboratories, and hospital portals with role-based access control.

### 3. Gen_AI Resume Processor
- **Role**: AI Engineer & UI Designer
- **Technology Stack**: React, TypeScript, Google Gemini API, Brutalist Design System
- **Key Achievements**: Created an automated resume generation and analysis engine utilizing Gemini LLM pipelines to parse career histories and tailor CV layouts in real-time.

### 4. CUDA (Privacy-First Local AI Assistant)
- **Role**: AI Automation Engineer
- **Technology Stack**: Python, Ollama, LangChain, Electron, Cross-Platform Desktop Automation
- **Key Achievements**: Built a 100% local desktop automation agent capable of file traversal, system actions, and contextual querying without transferring data to external servers.
